seoTitle: Record Customer Deposits in QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: Learn how to record customer deposits in QuickBooks Online to manage partial payments and keep cash flow accurate for your business.
intro: Accepting deposits from clients before starting a project is common for service-based businesses, but recording them correctly matters for accurate books. This guide covers how to record customer deposits in QuickBooks Online step by step.

steps.heading: Access the deposits feature
steps.body: Navigate to the area of QuickBooks Online where customer payments and deposits are recorded.

steps.heading: Record the deposit
steps.body: Enter the deposit amount against the correct customer, linking it to the relevant invoice or project if applicable.

steps.heading: Follow best practices
steps.body: Keep deposits clearly labeled so they're easy to apply against future invoices without confusion.

steps.heading: Avoid common mistakes
steps.body: Watch out for applying a deposit to the wrong invoice or forgetting to reconcile it once the final payment comes in.

faq.question: How do I apply a deposit to a final invoice?
faq.answer: Once the project or sale is complete, you can apply the recorded deposit as a credit against the final invoice amount.

faq.question: Can deposits be refunded if a project is canceled?
faq.answer: Yes, QuickBooks Online allows you to process a refund against a recorded deposit if the transaction needs to be reversed.

faq.question: Is recording a deposit different from recording a full payment?
faq.answer: Yes, a deposit represents a partial payment received in advance, while a full payment settles the entire invoice amount.

faq.question: Can this process be automated?
faq.answer: Automation features and integrations can help streamline recording deposits, especially for businesses handling frequent partial payments.
